entry.title: Re: Not able to use Workflow capability when starting App...
entry.content:
Hello Archana,

thanks for your answer. I added csrfProtection: false to my xs-app.json but the error is still the same. 

I also followed the post from korayyersel_q  in the comments of yout Blog (CF: bind a service to a locally run application") but this did also not help.

Any other ideas maybe?

Regards,

Juergen

entry.title: Re: how to combine/simplify these multiple IF Statements?
entry.content: @NISHANT_R your code is logically different than the code posted by the author.

entry.title: Re: Move files from SAP Application Server to MS teams Sh...
entry.content:
AL11 is not a "location", it's just a Gui that displays the files located on the file system of the application server! (So the files are still located in the standard Linux, Unix or Windows filesystem, depending on the OS on which the application server runs.)

With that in mind, we see that your question depends on the OS your ABAP system is running on. If it is running on Windows, you could simply create a share from the directory on Windows level and then link it in Teams as described here: How to share a (local network) folder/file link - Microsoft Community
(Be careful though with security concerns. It may not be a good idea to give too many people read or even write access to the file system of your ABAP server...)

If the ABAP system is running on Linux/Unix, this technique cannot be used. In that case (or if creating a network share on the application server is too insecure for your environment), you can use the following HTTP API for uploading files to MS Teams: Upload small files - Microsoft Graph v1.0 | Microsoft Learn

For example from an ABAP report:

  • Use OPEN DATASET to read the file from the file system
  • Then use CL_HTTP_CLIENT to call the above HTTP API in Teams

(However, this question is more a question about Microsoft technology than about SAP, isn't it?)

entry.title: Re: how to enter time for a terminated contingent worker?
entry.content:
Don´t get your point

"Work entries" are not entered in time off, but in the time sheet.

entry.title: Making Text Template mandatory in Charm
entry.content:
How to make Text Templates mandatory only for particular status of the change document? 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Making Text Template mandatory in Charm
entry.content:
Hello, 

You can make the texts/Partners/Dates mandatory using the customizing table "Specify Required Fields per Status" in View Cluster AIC_SETTINGS2.

The customizing table needs to be adjusted according to your needs to use this feature. Mark for each Transaction Type and User Status the fields, which you want to control. For Text, Partner and Date fields enter the specific values you would like to check in the following customizing tables:

  • Specify Required Texts per Status
  • Specify Required Partners per Status
  • Specify Required Dates per Status

If you would like to change the status of a CRM document via the PPF Action (e.g. "Set to in Development"), the system checks if all mandatory fields according to the customizing are filled. If not, you will get a message with the information which field is missing. The status won't be set in this case.

This information is provided in the Note 2849988 - Provide Status dependent check for mandatory fields in ChaRM

Best Regards, 
SAP Product Support

entry.title: Are you ready for Scope 3 reporting? Procurement in an ESG regulated world
entry.content:
Previously thought to be a voluntary responsibility, companies operating across the European Union (EU), Australia, Singapore and listed companies on China's three major stock exchanges are no longer immune to averting Scope 3 reporting obligations. 

The EU's Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD) since January 2023, and Australia's phased implementation of mandatory Scope 3 emissions reporting starting from the 1st of July 2024 - is expected to challenge many existing procurement professions both directly and indirectly worldwide. CSRD is estimated to impact approximately 50,000 European companies. 

When we talk about procurement as a service and operating function, preconceptions are evoked about the role of supply chain management purely being about spend control, supply chain resiliency, cost minimisation and compliance.

Indeed, procurement plays a significant role in asserting compliance and cost control. But what would this look like in an environmentally regulated landscape? One where environmentalism is no longer voluntary, but compulsory? 

With the rollout of CSRD in the EU, environmental, social and governance (ESG) principles are no longer about branding a company's tolerance and support for sustainability to appease stakeholders.

We're now entering an era of sustainable procurement compliance which is profoundly challenging our conventional presumptions on the role of procurement beyond simply purchasing.

Procurement leaders are starting to find that spend control and sustainable procurement compliance are mutually inclusive and supportive of one another.

Picture this, you are currently running a sourcing event and your objective is to minimise procured costs. By using DitchCarbon carbon risk ratings, you can select suppliers with a low emissions footprint, and therefore, potentially reduce your Scope 3 procured carbon tax. 

Carbon taxes will force finance, auditing and procurement professions to work together to tackle spend on emissions. Demonstrating decarbonisation starts at the supplier selection level and can only be done using accurate, auditable carbon values and calculations. An invoice with a carbon value, can be a good starting point for documenting these emissions. Scope 3 emissions calculators will become a critical part of the procurement ESG toolkit to help users approximate the emissions intensity of that item. In most cases, actual carbon emissions are always preferred over estimated ones because let's face it...who wants to overpay their carbon taxes!

However, collecting and reporting Scope 3 emissions is a significant challenge. Deloitte cites that the poor holistic integration of emissions collection technologies throughout a business's ecosystem, lack of tools especially from small to medium businesses to report Scope 3 emissions, poor supplier engagement, poor data quality and reliability, and rapidly evolving regulatory standards - all discourage many business leaders from investing in Scope 3 collection and reporting solutions...or even championing the cause at all. 

This was exemplified greatly in the SEC's recent policy backflip (March 2024) to make Scope 3 reporting voluntary. But bear in mind, that many of these SEC-listed, large companies will more than likely, still have to report their Scope 3 emissions if they have a major presence in the state of California (SB-253 Climate Corporate Data Accountability Act) or the EU. 

The Boston Consulting Group survey found that companies using automated digital emissions solutions were 2.5 times more likely to report their emissions comprehensively. Technology, therefore, is a huge enabler of sustainability compliance. 

Despite policy setbacks from the SEC, non-EU companies with a significant EU presence (activities greater than €150 million revenue turnover) are expected to report their Scope 3 emissions to the authorities. Most large US companies with a presence in the state of California are already mandated to report Scope 3 emissions. The Biden Administration is currently proposing to enforce mandatory Scope 3 emissions reporting for its major federal contractors (annual contract values of >$US50 million).

With supply chains contributing up to 88% of a company's emissions and 11.4 times that of Scope 1 and 2 primary emissions, it is no wonder that Scope 3 emissions reporting has garnered huge attention among regulators and environmental groups.

Fortunately, at SAP Ariba we are making strides and efforts to ensure that our customers remain compliant to Scope 3 reporting obligations and other ESG regulations. For example, enabling supplier Scope 3 carbon submissions and third-party sustainability certifications.

entry.title: Re: Problem with Select-Options
entry.content: Hi Raymond,

entry.title: Re: What are your SAP beginner tips?
entry.content:
Hi @sgk1991 and @MahmoodSonmari - welcome to SAP Community! If you are complete beginners to SAP - you might also want to join the Beginner Corner, and in particular read the blog post from David Chaviano as it includes a recommended SAP Learning Journey to get you started.

Be sure to read the following blog post also, to get started in SAP Community and take the tutorial tour!
Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!

Happy learning!

entry.title: Help with an import and update dataflow in SAP Gigya (Customer Data Cloud)
entry.content:
Hi, I am trying to build a dataflow that will allow me to load a new CSV from an FTP and update existing records in SAP Customer Data Cloud. In this source file, we will use email to match the records in SAP Customer Data Cloud  and then update the target fields.

We will have a range of different fields in our source file with the same target name, and we want to update the data exactly as is. We have already cleansed our source file to ensure the data matches the target. We will run this update on a regular basis (weekly). 

So far in the dataflow, I have setup the FTP activity, the field.rename activity (mapping source to target) but now I am unsure what activities are required next to handle the rest of the import. 

In the import, we would like to include logic that if the date of the data in the source file is less than the lastUpdate in Customer Data Cloud that we ignore the update. We are capturing the data in the source file using an online form and we are capturing a timestamp when it is updated, allowing us to use this timestamp for this logic. 

It would be great if anyone could point me in the direction of some test dataflows or example dataflows that have been setup for a similar use case. 

Our plan long term is to move towards flows and progressive profiling but for now we would like to setup an import to handle this. 

Thanks,
Mark

entry.title: Re: SAC: Do we need to define the properties of a dimensi...
entry.content:
Hi @skcpaleti65 , 

You need to create a propertie of type Text to use it in data action as a date. 

In my case I use this properties to calculate depreciation. So, in my data action, I can use DATEDIFF function between two dates or DAY function to count number of days in a month from a date in a propertie. 

I use dot between year, month and day but you should succeed without (to test).

 

I hope its helps you, 

Best Regards, 

Cynthia

Bests Regards, 

Cynthia

entry.title: Dunning Procedure
entry.content:
Hi all,
I am setting up a customized Dunning process in a partner demo environment.
Therefore, I assigned a value to the BP item “Dunning Procedure”.
However, the input values could not be saved due to the following error.

Error:
No dunning settings have been maintained for company code 1510

So I wanted to set up SSCUI 105758 (Manage Dunning Control for Company Codes).
However, this SSCUI is restricted to new entries and does not allow new company codes to be registered.
How can this problem be resolved?

entry.title: Re: SAC: Do we need to define the properties of a dimensi...
entry.content:
@skcpaleti65 

The type should be TEXT and the format should be as one of the below

YYYY-MM-DD, YYYYMMDD, YYYY/MM/DD, YYYY.MM.DD

Finally you should construct your logic something like below

IF DATEDIFF(TODAY(), [EmpID].[d/Sales_start_date], "YEAR") > 2 THEN
DATA()=RESULTLOOKUP() // Adjust as per your calculation logic
ENDIF

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Custom code analysis for SAP S/4HANA with SAP Fiori App Custom Code Migration
entry.content:
Hi @mainak-aich ,

here are the answers to your questions:

1. With this feature you can for example delete objects based on the old usage date.
2. Database analysis for SAP HANA is included in the checks of the custom code migration project for SAP S/4HANA, you don't need to run the FUNCTIONAL_DB and FUNCTIONAL_DB_ADDITION checks separately
3. PERFORMANCE_DB checks are not included in the checks of the custom code migration project for SAP S/4HANA. Please be aware, that these checks should only be used as an additional help after performance analysis with SQL monitor to fix the issues found in the SQL monitor.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Where in S4H enter the old number from legacy ERP?
entry.content:
Greetings,

you can use "Old material number" field in Basic data 1:

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Re: What is serialisation in equipment & functional locat...
entry.content:
Greetings, 

generally you use serial numbers  if you want to link PM objects with inventory management. You would use this if you want to move individual pieces of equipment trough stock postings (for example MIGO).

Serial numbers can also be used without PM object linked. In such an example you uniquely identify each unit of a material by its serial number.

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: Re: SAC story design exam(C_SACS_2321) expiring soon, wha...
entry.content: Yes, we will have C_SAC_2408 in June.

entry.title: Re: link chart and table in SAC
entry.content:
Hello @krishnakumar_nagarajan01 , 

To link your chart with your table you need to use Linked Analysis Option.

Option > Linked Analysis > Settings 

Only Selected Widgets > Select your table 

Select : Filter on Data Point selection

I hope its helps you, 

Best Regards, 

Cynthia

Margit

entry.title: Re: Where to define the partners list (person responsible...
entry.content:
Greetings,

you need to define a partner determination procedure:

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Plant Maintenance and Customer Service -> Maintenance and Service Processing -> Maintenance and Service Orders -> Partner -> Define Partner Determination Procedure and Partner Function

You need to link this procedure to order type. Partner functions can be transferred from technical object or from notification if the same partner determination is used.

Best regards, Matjaž.

entry.title: SAC F4 help new lines with hierarchy visualization not working drill down
entry.content:
I am working with an SAC planning table based on a model BPC live connection on a BW version 7.5 SP28. When I add a new member to introduce new lines in the table it is working fine, but if I would like to see the F4 input help when the hierarchy is active I am not able to drill down in the hierarchy. I see only the root hierarchy node, but nothing else. If I use the searching cell without using the match code the search works fine, but I would like to know if it is possible to use the search with the hierarchy activated in the match code by displaying the hierarchy.

I checked the SAP note https://me.sap.com/notes/3238379 , but in my case versión BW 7.5 SP28 already has those patches implemented.

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: how to combine/simplify these multiple IF Statements?
entry.content: @NISHANT_R Nope, your code will produce different result than the code posted by the author.

entry.title: Re: how to combine/simplify these multiple IF Statements?
entry.content: You are using ELSEIF which is not used in OPs code.

br
edgar

entry.title: Re: MMPV - Implications of opening next period +1 during...
entry.content:
Hello @jaytee 

That process might bite you when you convert to S/4HANA where material ledger is obligatory for valuated materials:

  • Simplification item S4TWL - Material Ledger Obligatory for Material Valuation
  • Business Impact Note 2267834 - S4TWL - Material Ledger Obligatory for Material Valuation

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: How to get all logs or list of all events triggered t...
entry.content:
Hi,


Your function needs to produce logs. 
Serverless server wrapper for you function code does not produce logs on its own whenever function is called.

The easiest would be to include logs in your function code, i.e 
```
console.log("Function triggered with payload", event.data)
```
Then, after function is redeployed, it will produce logs which you can inspect via kubectl
```
kubectl logs -l serverless.kyma-project.io/function-name=my-function
```

Best Regards
Krzysztof

entry.title: Re: How to apply a function to fetch data from a user?
entry.content:
You can try Table_1.getDataSource().setDimensionFilter. 

Refer to the SAC Reference API guide:

Analytics Designer API Reference Guide Version 2024.2 (sap.com)

entry.title: 📅 SAP S/4HANA Beraterausbildung ab 10. Juni 2024
entry.content:
Wussten Sie schon, dass Sie Ihre Weiterbildung ganz einfach berufsbegleitend machen können?

Mit der SAP Blended Learning Academy treiben Sie Ihre Karriere mit einer gefragten SAP Zertifizierung voran - die kommende Sommerzeit eignet sich ideal, um sich der persönlichen und beruflichen Weiterentwicklung zu widmen.

Dabei lernen Sie selbstständig wann und wo Sie wollen, ohne auf den persönlichen Kontakt und regelmäßigen Austausch mit Dozenten und Mitlernenden verzichten zu müssen.

In wenigen Wochen geht´s los - sichern Sie sich jetzt einen Platz:

SAP S/4HANA Consultant Academy:

  • WS4HFI Financial Accounting in SAP S/4HANA (FI)*
  • WS4HCO Management Accounting in SAP S/4HANA (CO)
  • WS4HPP Manufacturing in SAP S/4HANA (PP)
  • WS4HMM Procurement in SAP S/4HANA (MM)
  • WS4HSD Sales in SAP S/4HANA (SD)*

SAP S/4HANA Integration Academy:

  • WS4INT Integrated Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA*

*Garantierte Durchführung

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Fiori My Inbox – Part 1 Activation
entry.content:

Thanks

Shankar

entry.title: Re: How to get all logs or list of all events triggered t...
entry.content:
With https://github.com/kyma-project/serverless/releases/tag/1.4.3 we have patched the problematic configuration of secrets, unfortunately the warning event will stop be emitted by kubernetes after function pod restart. You can restart all the function pods in a given namespace by running

kubectl rollout restart deployment -l serverless.kyma-project.io/managed-by=function-controller -n {namespace}

entry.title: Re: SAC how to get performance form rating data from succ...
entry.content:
Hello Team,

To fetch any data from SF to SAC we have to first create a connection. For that you can follow this below KBA-

2339542 - How to connect to SAP SuccessFactors from SAP Analytics Cloud.

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2628523

entry.title: Re: Delay Learning Assignments
entry.content:
Hi Santiago

At present this particular functionality is currently not available. However there is an enhancement request created for this issue which has the status of For Long-term Consideration

Please feel free to add you comment. Also a similar enhancement has been Acknowledged by Product Management but again no time line on when this may be available.

If this information helped you then please consider giving Kudos or accepting this post as solution.

Thanks

Padraig

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 4 - Mobile device capabilities
entry.content: @anumalasingh Had similar last week using a business e-mail address and had to raise a support ticket with foursquare but they can take a day to fix. Alternatively, try using a gmail account.

entry.title: Re: visual code studio command run failed with error : sp...
entry.content:
Hello,

I am happy to announce that the fix is now released. The fix is contained in "@sap/ux-ui5-tooling" package, version 1.13.3. In order to obtain the fix, please do the following:

  1. In VSCode open the command palette
  2. Search and execute the command "Fiori: Open Application Info"
  3. After the Application Info Page is opened, click on the link for updating "@sap/ux-ui5-tooling"

Best regards,

Zdravko

entry.title: Re: visual code studio command run failed with error : sp...
entry.content: Hi, this issue is now fixed. Please check my answer above.

entry.title: New runtime (4.9.259) issue
entry.content:
Hi there!

I am facing this problem with all builds done with the new Client runtime version 4.9.259: my app gets frozen on the login page.

This is what is reported by the Google Play Console:

java.lang.RuntimeException: Unable to start activity ComponentInfo{xxx.com/xxx.com.MainActivity}: androidx.fragment.app.Fragment$InstantiationException: Unable to instantiate fragment com.swmansion.rnscreens.ScreenFragment: calling Fragment constructor caused an exception

 

Has the same thing happened to anyone?

entry.title: Re: Linking into several variants from diagrams in Signav...
entry.content:
Hello Anna,

I understand your point and concern.  Process variants and templates are distinct process models within the repository. Assisting users in navigating this repository can be approached through various methods, such as employing navigation maps or establishing links between process models and variants. Another potential approach could involve implementing a diagram landing page tailored to the plants or locations of users within the HUB, ensuring that they encounter relevant diagrams upon accessing Signavio. For the most effective strategy implementation, it's advisable to seek guidance from our professional services team, who can advise on the current range of possibilities and best practices. You can address this request to the following email: signavio_success@sap.com

Best,

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the SAP Enterprise Architecture Community Group!
entry.content:
Happy to be part of this group.

Thanks and Regards,

Kirti Singh

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP Build – Seamless Integration of Processes and Apps
entry.content: Does the free tier plan of BPA support API Triggering?

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hi Community,
Excited to be here!!!

entry.title: Re: SAP WEBI how to generate 20 week date range during first day of month and last day of month
entry.content: Did you already have the generated date and week in your webi and only had to create the new intervals by the end of the month?

entry.title: Re: How to sort Spaces and Pages in SAP Build Work Zone,...
entry.content:
Hi Vera,

We are also waiting for this feature.
What is the current status?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 4 - Mobile device capabilities
entry.content: Excellent job with homepage and Details page calculations. However, the hash doesnt seem to be right. Could you look into the formula editor for the name of the object that we are looking for?

entry.title: Re: Access Request role search restriction using GRAC_ROL...
entry.content:
Dear Nandita, 

Maybe you are trying to something that goes not incompatible SAP's selection criteria. 

I kindly recommend that you manually add a new object to the role and enter the second value into that newly added object.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: SAP Community - Where are you from?
entry.content:
Hello, I am living in Paris area in France. In about 3 months the Olympic games will start here...

I am working as Concur technical consultant at SAP.

entry.title: Change payment terms and payment method vendor invoice
entry.content:
Dear All,

when we post a supplier invoice and we change the payment terms, the payment method does not change automatically even if in customizing are linked. Is it a SAP standard behaviour?

Thanks and Kind Regards.

Gloria

entry.title: ABAP2XLSX change comment size
entry.content:
Hello.

I'm using ABAP2XLSX to create some excel files.

I need to add comments in some cells.
I've seen the example ZDEMO_EXCEL_COMMENTS and I've managed to create comments in my file.

The problem is that the size of the comment is too small. And I cannot see any option in the method add_comment to change the size.

So I'm trying to change the size of all the comments added by code (by the moment).
In class ZCL_EXCEL_WRITER_2007 and method CREATE_XL_DRAWING_FOR_COMMENTS I've seen a line to create the shape of the comment and the following line:


lc_xml_attr_style       value = 'size:auto;width:auto;height:auto;position:absolute;margin-left:117pt;margin-top:172.5pt;z-index:1;visibility:hidden' ),

I'm trying to change the width and height and some other values in this line, buy nothing happens.

I've tried removing "size:auto"
and put "width:900pt;height:40pt "

Any tip?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Pricing procedure determination for Free of Charge pr...
entry.content: In your reply 'A' represents document pricing procedure. It is standard document pricing procedure. Free of charge documents usually have another one. I can find it in CBC in the app ID 101118 (Set pricing precedure determination).

entry.title: Re: CPM Feedback request card. Mobile enabled?
entry.content: Looking at this again. It does note in the guide that it is not mobile enabled.

entry.title: Incorrect stock in MD04
entry.content:
Hi,

After S/4 implementation MD04 stock are incorrect. We have 5 stock provided to vendor and 5 stock in unrestricted.

But MD04 is displaying stock as 10. MD04 should show only unrestricted stock.

I am using MRP area type 01.

We don't want to use subcontract MRP type.

entry.title: BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEREPLICA Extension
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have a requirement to update custom fields in the material master through the custom program. I am trying to use BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEREPLICA BAPI by passing extension parameters. The Return parameter is giving success message but the data is not updating. Its gives me like No changes made message.

Anyone faced this issue and have any solution for this ? Please let me know. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Hi, I am Gaayathri from Bangalore. Happy to be part of this community.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Dear David,

I am Lesia Bilohan, and I would like to express my keen interest in securing a free certification attempt under your esteemed initiative. I am greatly inspired by your commitment to empowering underrepresented groups in the technology field.

Originally from Ukraine, I left it in 2022 I am now building my life and restart my professional journey in the Netherlands. My professional background spans B2B sales and business development in the IT and fintech sectors. After taking a career break I am currently reskilling and upskilling myself to be able to make a pivot in my professional journey and become a technology professional with focus on data analytics. My role has involved strategic planning, customer relationship management, and leading market expansion efforts on an international scale. These experiences have sharpened my analytical abilities and deepened my understanding of business and operational risks, sparking my interest in SAP’s ERP systems.

Motivated to immerse myself in the SAP ecosystem, I am eager to contribute to its vibrant and diverse community. I recognize the critical importance of certifications in the tech industry as they not only validate expertise but also open doors to new professional opportunities and advancements.

 This opportunity would be invaluable for both my personal growth and professional development, and it would mark a significant step toward overcoming barriers and promoting diversity within the industry.

I am excited about the possibility of joining the SAP community and leveraging my diverse skill set for positive contributions. Committed to continuous learning, I am enthusiastic about participating in projects that meld technology with strategic business solutions.

Thank you for considering my application. 

Warm regards,

Lesia Bilohan

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Welcome @KirtiSingh01 and @Gaayathri - be sure to check out our location groups!

@Gaayathri - you might like to join the Bengaluru group, if you haven't done so already.

entry.title: Managed Systems Setup Check for On-Premise ABAP systems connected to SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
The ABAP system Managed Systems Setup Check now available for customers.
This is a tool you can use to troubleshoot problems with Managed ABAP systems connected to SAP Cloud ALM

It is included in ST-PI 740 SP26, and for lower supported ST-PI versions via SAP Notes 3426408 - Cloud ALM: Connection Setup Checks for Deployment Management and 3432845 - SAP Cloud ALM - CDM: Create DDIC objects needed for note 3426408

We want to encourage customers to use this tool 

It has been implemented via transaction /n/sdf/alm_diagnostic, or via report /SDF/CALM_CDM_CONN_DIAGNOSTIC and should be used as the first step when having issues with the Managed ABAP system.

As you can see there are various checks and the solutions can be found in the Solutions for Errors in the Managed System Setup Check here

Here is an example run in one of SAP's Test ERP 6.0 Test Systems where it has found an issue and the link to the solution was used to navigate to it in Help SAP

Again this is a tool customers can use to help diagnose issues  on the managed ABAP system and enable customers to resolve many of them on their own using the tool and the embedded links to SAP Help to find the solution based on the check status warning/error.

entry.title: Re: Incorrect stock in MD04
entry.content:
Hi,

Please see here

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/stock-with-vendor/qaq-p/12082517

Regards

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud and On-Premise Deployment Options
entry.content:
Hello Amin & members,

Very good insight.
I particularly wanted to know whether having the HANA database on premise and application servers on the cloud is an option (in line with GDPR and other compliance requirements).
Is there any such offering/option from SAP?

Also, is there any latest (2024) documentation/video from SAP with respect to the different cloud options?

Suraj

entry.title: Re: I created a table function, but in amdp I am not able to create table using sqlscript to append
entry.content: As I know, in AMDP you cannot create tables dynamically like you do in SQL. Did you check, if you can use table variables to store and manipulate data?

entry.title: Re: SAP Hana Calculation view input parameter from JPA Entity
entry.content: Did you check, if you can pass the input parameter to the calculation view by using the `setParameter` method of the `Query` object in JPA?
